server.h(3) Library Functions Manual server.h(3)

NAME

server.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acds_client_data_t
Per-client connection data. struct migration_context_t
In-memory host migration context. struct acds_server_t
Discovery server state.

Functions


asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *server, const acds_config_t *config)
Initialize discovery server. asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *server)
Run discovery server main loop. void acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t *server)
Shutdown discovery server. void * acds_client_handler (void *arg)
Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). void * acds_websocket_client_handler (void *arg)
WebSocket client handler (thread entry point).

Function Documentation

void * acds_client_handler (void * arg)

Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP packets from a connected client. Handles crypto handshake, then dispatches packets to session/signaling handlers.

Parameters

arg Pointer to client connection context

Returns

NULL (thread exit value)

Definition at line 916 of file src/discovery-service/server.c.

916                                      {
917   tcp_client_context_t *ctx = (tcp_client_context_t *)arg;
918   if (!ctx) {
919     log_error("Client handler: NULL context");
920     return NULL;
921   }
922 
923   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
924   socket_t client_socket = ctx->client_socket;
925 
926   // Get client IP for logging
927   char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN] = {0};
928   tcp_client_context_get_ip(ctx, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip));
929 
930   log_info("Client handler started for %s", client_ip);
931 
932   // Register client in TCP server registry with allocated client data
933   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
934   if (!client_data) {
935     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to allocate client data");
936     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
937     return NULL;
938   }
939   memset(client_data, 0, sizeof(*client_data));
940   client_data->joined_session = false;
941   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
942 
943   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
944   char crypto_name[96];
945   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_client_%s", client_ip);
946   asciichat_error_t handshake_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
947   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
948     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %s", client_ip);
949     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
950     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
951     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
952     return NULL;
953   }
954 
955   // Set server identity keys for handshake
956   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
957   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
958   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
959   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
960 
961   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
962     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
963     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to register client in registry");
964     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
965     return NULL;
966   }
967 
968   log_debug("Client %s registered (socket=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, client_socket,
969             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
970 
971   // Create transport for handshake and subsequent client communication
972   acip_transport_t *transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("acds_tcp_client", client_socket, NULL);
973   if (!transport) {
974     log_error("Failed to create TCP transport for client %s", client_ip);
975     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
976     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
977     return NULL;
978   }
979 
980   // Perform crypto handshake (three-step process)
981   log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with client %s", client_ip);
982 
983   // Step 0: Send crypto parameters
984   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
985   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
986     log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed for client %s", client_ip);
987     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
988     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
989     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
990     return NULL;
991   }
992 
993   // Step 1: Start handshake (send server key, receive client key)
994   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_start(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
995   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
996     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed for client %s", client_ip);
997     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
998     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
999     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1000     return NULL;
1001   }
1002 
1003   // Step 2: Authentication challenge (if required)
1004   {
1005     packet_type_t packet_type;
1006     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
1007     size_t payload_len = 0;
1008     int recv_result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
1009     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1010       log_warn("Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE from client %s", client_ip);
1011       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1012       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1013       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1014       return NULL;
1015     }
1016     handshake_result =
1017         crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
1018     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1019   }
1020   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1021     log_warn("Crypto handshake auth challenge failed for client %s", client_ip);
1022     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1023     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1024     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1025     return NULL;
1026   }
1027 
1028   // Step 3: Complete handshake (verify and finalize) - skip if already complete
1029   if (client_data->handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1030     packet_type_t packet_type = 0;
1031     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
1032     size_t payload_len = 0;
1033     int recv_result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
1034     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1035       log_warn("Failed to receive AUTH_RESPONSE from client %s", client_ip);
1036       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1037       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1038       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1039       return NULL;
1040     }
1041     handshake_result =
1042         crypto_handshake_server_complete(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
1043     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1044     if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1045       log_warn("Crypto handshake complete failed for client %s", client_ip);
1046       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1047       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1048       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1049       return NULL;
1050     }
1051   } else {
1052     log_debug("Crypto handshake already complete from auth_challenge step for client %s", client_ip);
1053   }
1054 
1055   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1056   log_info("Crypto handshake complete for client %s", client_ip);
1057 
1058   // Store transport and registry ID in client data, and link back from transport
1059   client_data->transport = transport;
1060   client_data->registry_id = client_socket;
1061   transport->user_data = client_data;
1062 
1063   // Main packet processing loop
1064   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1065     packet_type_t packet_type;
1066     void *payload = NULL;
1067     size_t payload_size = 0;
1068 
1069     // Receive packet (blocking with system timeout)
1070     int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_size);
1071 
1072     if (result >= 0) {
1073       log_info("★ ACDS_CLIENT_HANDLER: Received packet type=%u from %s, payload_size=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1074     }
1075 
1076     if (result < 0) {
1077       // Check error context to distinguish timeout from actual disconnect
1078       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1079       bool has_context = HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx);
1080 
1081       // Check if this is a timeout (non-fatal) or actual disconnect (fatal)
1082       asciichat_error_t error = GET_ERRNO();
1083       if (error == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT ||
1084           (error == ERROR_NETWORK && has_context && strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "timed out") != NULL)) {
1085         // Timeout waiting for next packet - this is normal, continue waiting
1086         log_debug("Client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1087         if (payload) {
1088           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1089         }
1090         continue;
1091       }
1092 
1093       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1094       log_info("Client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1095       if (payload) {
1096         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1097       }
1098       break;
1099     }
1100 
1101     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1102 
1103     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1104     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1105       bool allowed =
1106           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1107 
1108       if (!allowed) {
1109         log_warn("Client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG "
1110                  "allowed",
1111                  client_ip, packet_type);
1112 
1113         // Send error response using persistent transport
1114         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1115                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1116 
1117         // Free payload and continue
1118         if (payload) {
1119           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1120         }
1121         continue;
1122       }
1123     }
1124 
1125     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1126     // Set server context for callbacks
1127     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1128     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1129 
1130     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1131         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1132 
1133     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1134       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1135                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1136     }
1137 
1138     // Free payload (allocated by receive_packet via buffer_pool_alloc)
1139     if (payload) {
1140       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1141     }
1142 
1143     // Keep handler running after session_established
1144     // Handler continues to receive and relay WebRTC signaling packets (SDP, ICE)
1145     // Broadcasts don't go through this socket - they use tcp_server_foreach_client() to write directly
1146   }
1147 
1148   // Cleanup - handler always cleans up since it runs until connection closes
1149   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1150   log_debug("Client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1151 
1152   // Destroy transport before closing socket
1153   acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1154   socket_close(client_socket);
1155 
1156   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1157 
1158   log_info("Client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1159   return NULL;
1160 }

    acip_transport_destroy(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,

    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx, HAS_ERRNO,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,

    tcp_client_context_get_ip(), acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),

    tcp_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_server_init().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *
  server, const acds_config_t * config)


Initialize discovery server. Loads or generates identity keys,
    opens database, creates session registry, and binds TCP socket.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Configuration from command-line
  parsing


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 191 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


191                                                                                        {
192   if (!server || !config) {
193     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
194   }
195 
196   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
197   memcpy(&server->config, config, sizeof(acds_config_t));
198 
199   // Open database (SQLite as single source of truth)
200   asciichat_error_t result = database_init(config->database_path, &server->db);
201   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
202     return result;
203   }
204 
205   // Initialize rate limiter with SQLite backend
206   server->rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_sqlite(NULL); // NULL = externally managed DB
207   if (!server->rate_limiter) {
208     database_close(server->db);
209     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create rate limiter");
210   }
211 
212   // Set the database handle for the rate limiter
213   rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(server->rate_limiter, server->db);
214 
215   // Configure TCP server
216   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
217       .port = config->port,
218       .ipv4_address = (config->address[0] != '\0') ? config->address : NULL,
219       .ipv6_address = (config->address6[0] != '\0') ? config->address6 : NULL,
220       .bind_ipv4 = (config->address[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
221       .bind_ipv6 = (config->address6[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
222       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
223       .client_handler = acds_client_handler,
224       .user_data = server,
225   };
226 
227   // Initialize TCP server
228   result = tcp_server_init(&server->tcp_server, &tcp_config);
229   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
230     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
231     database_close(server->db);
232     return result;
233   }
234 
235   // Initialize background worker thread pool
236   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, false);
237   server->worker_pool = thread_pool_create("acds_workers");
238   if (!server->worker_pool) {
239     log_warn("Failed to create worker thread pool");
240     tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
241     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
242     database_close(server->db);
243     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create worker thread pool");
244   }
245 
246   // Spawn cleanup thread in worker pool
247   if (thread_pool_spawn(server->worker_pool, cleanup_thread_func, server, 0, "cleanup") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
248     log_warn("Failed to spawn cleanup thread (continuing without cleanup)");
249   }
250 
251   log_info("Discovery server initialized successfully");
252   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
253 }

    acds_config_t::address, acds_config_t::address6,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(),

    acds_server_t::db, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, acds_config_t::port,
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_create_sqlite(),
    rate_limiter_destroy(), rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(),
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::shutdown,
    acds_server_t::tcp_server, tcp_server_destroy(),
    tcp_server_init(), thread_pool_create(),
    thread_pool_spawn(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *
  server)


Run discovery server main loop. Accepts client connections and
    spawns handler threads. Blocks until shutdown signal received.




Parameters


server Initialized server structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 255 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


255                                                          {
256   if (!server) {
257     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
258   }
259 
260   log_info("Discovery server accepting connections on port %d", server->config.port);
261 
262   // Delegate to TCP server abstraction
263   return tcp_server_run(&server->tcp_server);
264 }






References acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_info, acds_config_t::port,
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::tcp_server, and
    tcp_server_run().


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t * server)


Shutdown discovery server. Closes listen socket, stops accepting
    connections, waits for handler threads to exit, closes database, and frees
    resources.




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 266 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


266                                                  {
267   if (!server) {
268     return;
269   }
270 
271   // Signal shutdown to worker threads
272   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, true);
273 
274   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets, stops accept loop)
275   tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
276 
277   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit
278   size_t remaining_clients;
279   int shutdown_attempts = 0;
280   const int max_shutdown_attempts = 100; // 10 seconds (100 * 100ms)
281 
282   while ((remaining_clients = tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server)) > 0 &&
283          shutdown_attempts < max_shutdown_attempts) {
284     log_debug("Waiting for %zu client handler threads to exit (attempt %d/%d)", remaining_clients,
285               shutdown_attempts + 1, max_shutdown_attempts);
286     platform_sleep_ms(100);
287     shutdown_attempts++;
288   }
289 
290   if (remaining_clients > 0) {
291     log_warn("Server shutdown: %zu client handler threads still running after 10 seconds", remaining_clients);
292   } else if (shutdown_attempts > 0) {
293     log_debug("All client handler threads exited gracefully");
294   }
295 
296   // Stop and destroy worker thread pool (cleanup thread, etc.)
297   if (server->worker_pool) {
298     thread_pool_destroy(server->worker_pool);
299     server->worker_pool = NULL;
300     log_debug("Worker thread pool stopped");
301   }
302 
303   // Destroy rate limiter
304   if (server->rate_limiter) {
305     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
306     server->rate_limiter = NULL;
307   }
308 
309   // Close database
310   if (server->db) {
311     database_close(server->db);
312     server->db = NULL;
313   }
314 
315   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
316 }






References atomic_store_bool(), database_close(),
    acds_server_t::db, log_debug, log_info,
    log_warn, platform_sleep_ms(),
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_destroy(),
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_destroy(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    thread_pool_destroy(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  * acds_websocket_client_handler (void * arg)


WebSocket client handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP
    packets from a WebSocket client connection. Similar to TCP handler but uses
    transport-based APIs for crypto handshake.




Parameters


arg Pointer to
  websocket_client_context_t


Returns


NULL (thread exit value)


Definition at line 1166 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


1166                                                {
1167   websocket_client_context_t *ctx = (websocket_client_context_t *)arg;
1168   if (!ctx) {
1169     log_error("WebSocket client handler: NULL context");
1170     return NULL;
1171   }
1172 
1173   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
1174   acip_transport_t *transport = ctx->transport;
1175   char *client_ip = ctx->client_ip;
1176 
1177   log_info("WebSocket client handler started for %s", client_ip);
1178 
1179   // Allocate client_data
1180   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
1181   if (!client_data) {
1182     log_error("Failed to allocate client data for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1183     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1184     return NULL;
1185   }
1186 
1187   client_data->transport = transport;
1188   client_data->joined_session = false;
1189   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
1190   transport->user_data = client_data;
1191 
1192   // Generate synthetic socket ID for tcp_server registry
1193   static _Atomic int g_ws_synthetic_id = INT_MAX;
1194   int synthetic_id = atomic_fetch_sub(&g_ws_synthetic_id, 1);
1195   client_data->registry_id = synthetic_id;
1196 
1197   // Register in tcp_server with synthetic ID (so callbacks can find client_data)
1198   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1199     log_error("Failed to register WebSocket client %s in registry", client_ip);
1200     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
1201     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1202     return NULL;
1203   }
1204 
1205   log_debug("WebSocket client %s registered (synthetic_id=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, synthetic_id,
1206             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1207 
1208   // Determine if client authentication is required by checking server configuration
1209   bool auth_required = server->config.require_client_identity;
1210   ctx->auth_required = auth_required;
1211 
1212   // Skip crypto handshake only for wss:// connections WITHOUT authentication requirement
1213   // For plain ws://, always do crypto handshake
1214   // For wss:// WITH auth requirement, always do crypto handshake
1215   bool skip_handshake = ctx->is_secure && !auth_required;
1216 
1217   if (skip_handshake) {
1218     client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1219     log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - skipping crypto handshake (TLS encryption used, no auth required)",
1220              client_ip);
1221   } else {
1222     log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - proceeding with crypto handshake%s", client_ip,
1223              auth_required ? " (client authentication required)" : "");
1224   }
1225 
1226   // Main packet processing loop using transport recv
1227   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1228     void *recv_buffer = NULL;
1229     size_t recv_len = 0;
1230     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1231 
1232     asciichat_error_t recv_result = acip_transport_recv(transport, &recv_buffer, &recv_len, &alloc_buffer);
1233     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1234       // Check if this is a timeout or disconnect
1235       if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1236         log_debug("WebSocket client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1237         if (alloc_buffer) {
1238           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1239         }
1240         continue;
1241       }
1242 
1243       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1244       log_info("WebSocket client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1245       if (alloc_buffer) {
1246         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1247       }
1248       break;
1249     }
1250 
1251     // Parse packet header from received data
1252     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1253       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: received packet too small (%zu bytes)", client_ip, recv_len);
1254       if (alloc_buffer) {
1255         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1256       }
1257       continue;
1258     }
1259 
1260     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)recv_buffer;
1261     packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1262     size_t payload_size = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1263     const void *payload = (const uint8_t *)recv_buffer + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1264 
1265     // Validate payload size
1266     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size) {
1267       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: packet size mismatch (expected %zu, got %zu)", client_ip,
1268                sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size, recv_len);
1269       if (alloc_buffer) {
1270         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1271       }
1272       continue;
1273     }
1274 
1275     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1276 
1277     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1278     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1279       bool allowed =
1280           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1281 
1282       if (!allowed) {
1283         log_warn("WebSocket client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only "
1284                  "SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed",
1285                  client_ip, packet_type);
1286         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1287                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1288         if (alloc_buffer) {
1289           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1290         }
1291         continue;
1292       }
1293     }
1294 
1295     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1296     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1297     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1298 
1299     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1300         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1301 
1302     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1303       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1304                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1305     }
1306 
1307     if (alloc_buffer) {
1308       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1309     }
1310   }
1311 
1312   // Cleanup
1313   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1314   log_debug("WebSocket client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1315 
1316   // Transport is owned by WebSocket server, don't destroy it here
1317 
1318   log_info("WebSocket client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1319 
1320   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1321 
1322   return NULL;
1323 }






References acip_handle_acds_packet(),
    acip_send_error(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_client_context_t::auth_required, buffer_pool_free(),
    websocket_client_context_t::client_ip, acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    websocket_client_context_t::is_secure,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE, PACKET_TYPE_PING,
    PACKET_TYPE_PONG, acds_client_data_t::registry_id,
    acds_config_t::require_client_identity, tcp_server::running,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_remove_client(), acds_client_data_t::transport,
    websocket_client_context_t::transport, packet_header_t::type,
    acip_transport::user_data, and
    websocket_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main().

    Version 0.9.13
    ascii-chat
